page: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Bpsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an ARM64 architecture powered by an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The configuration includes 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading, resulting in 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a performance profile that leans toward the lower to average tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests yield average results of 1822.69 and 7252.95, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show an average performance of 46.07 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 29.04 GB/sec for large block write operations, though small block cached reads are weak at 282.3 GB/sec. Geekbench scores are generally weak, with a single-core score of 1109.0 and a multi-core score of 3672.0. Passmark database operations score in the average tier at 2889.77, while cryptographic performance is poor, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 647.61, despite hardware AES support.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the B4ps_v2 utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, making it a resource-dense option for workloads with variable CPU utilization. The primary tradeoff is the burstable nature of the CPU and the weak performance in compute-heavy tasks, such as LLM inference, where text generation for a small 135M model is poor at 40.15 tokens/sec. This server is best suited for low-traffic web serving, lightweight database operations, basic text processing, and development or testing environments that do not require sustained high CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Bpsv2 family, utilizing an ARM64 Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 4 physical cores, 4 vCPUs, and 16.0 GB of RAM, with no bundled local storage or GPUs. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, its performance profile is characterized by average stress-ng CPU metrics but weak overall Geekbench and Passmark scores. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks but weak for small cached blocks. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and qualitative cost efficiency, it is suited for workloads with variable demands, such as lightweight database operations, text processing, and low-traffic web serving, rather than sustained compute-heavy tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor, designed for cost-efficient workloads with variable CPU utilization.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B4ps_v2 is a burstable ARM64 virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable ARM64 virtual machine with balanced memory and variable CPU allocation for cost-conscious, non-sustained workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor with 4 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for variable workload demands
No bundled local storage, but supports Premium Storage options
Best suited for lightweight database operations, text processing, and low-traffic web serving
